facts.text: Hi‑Nella’s name comes from a Native American term meaning “high rolling knoll” or “high ground,” though some say it honors Nella Parker.
The borough was officially created on April 23, 1929, carved from portions of the former Clementon Township alongside four other new municipalities.
Hi‑Nella ranks as New Jersey’s ninth‑smallest municipality, a fact that highlights its tight‑knit community feel.
Like most New Jersey towns, Hi‑Nella operates under the borough form of government, the state’s most common municipal structure.
In 2020, Hi‑Nella’s property tax rate hit 5.306%, the fifth‑highest in the state, far above the county average.
The borough shares borders with Gloucester Township, Somerdale, and Stratford, situating it amid a cluster of Camden County communities.
